home.headline: Explore Human Anatomy at
home.value_prop: Interactive 3D visualization provides a profound understanding of the human body. Gain valuable insights into organ systems, identify early biomarkers, and explore complex systemic conditions through our comprehensive, medically-accurate 3D platform.
home.why_subtitle: Our platform goes beyond traditional 2D textbooks, offering an unprecedented level of clinical depth and interactivity.
home.feat1_desc: Seamlessly transition from macro organ structures to microscopic cellular activity and neurotransmitter pathways.
home.feat2_desc: Visualize early signs of oxidative stress, toxins, and autoimmune markers before clinical diagnosis.
home.feat3_desc: Interact with highly detailed 3D models of the brain, heart, liver, digestive tract, and DNA.

about.title: Bridging the Gap Between
about.subtitle: SomaLab is an interactive digital anatomy and health educational platform designed to provide a deep, accurate, and accessible visualization of the human body's structures and functions.
about.story_p1: This platform was created to bridge the gap between complex medical information and general public comprehension. We believe that knowledge of one's own body is the first step toward a healthier lifestyle.
about.story_p2: SomaLab brings modern 3D technology to your fingertips—empowering students, educators, medical professionals, and the general public to explore the wonders of the human body without limits.
about.pillars_subtitle: The foundations that drive our platform forward.
about.vision_desc: To be the leading interactive anatomy learning platform that democratizes medical knowledge, enabling individuals from all backgrounds to better recognize and understand their bodies.
about.mission_item1: Present accurate yet lightweight and interactive 3D medical models.
about.mission_item2: Transform rigid biology learning into a fun exploration experience.
about.mission_item3: Provide reliable science-based information to improve public health literacy.
about.why_desc: Textbooks and 2D illustrations are often insufficient to represent the complexity of a living, moving body. SomaLab was built to create a virtual laboratory where your curiosity can be answered visually, in detail, and in real-time.

contact.title: Get in
contact.subtitle: Have questions about our medical visualization platform or interested in collaboration? We'd love to hear from you.
contact.placeholder_msg: How can we help you today?

footer.desc: Empowering preventative healthcare through immersive 3D clinical visualization. Bridging the gap between complex medical science and accessible public knowledge.

neural.title: Comprehensive Neurological Autoimmunity & Brain Health Assessment
neural.desc1: The Neural Zoomer Plus evaluates immune reactivity to neurological antigens associated with neuroinflammation, cognitive decline, and autoimmune-related brain disorders.
neural.desc2: This test identifies autoimmune activity years before clinical diagnosis, enabling early intervention when treatments are most effective. By detecting both autoantibodies and infectious triggers, it provides a complete picture of neuroinflammation causes.
neural.desc3: The panel is particularly valuable for patients with unexplained neurological symptoms, helping differentiate between various conditions and guide targeted therapeutic approaches.
symptoms: Memory loss, brain fog, or cognitive decline
Family history of Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, or Multiple Sclerosis
markers: 48 neurological antigens including anti-myelin, anti-ganglioside antibodies
Alzheimer's markers: Anti-amyloid beta, anti-tau antibodies
Neurotransmitter receptor antibodies: Dopamine, glutamate, GABA receptors
Infections that most commonly act as the trigger, including Group A Strep, Epstein Barr Virus
Cytomegalovirus, and the Herpes virus family
Optional ApoE genotype testing for Alzheimer's risk
highlights: Utilizing advanced bio-chip microarray technology, this panel provides a deeper analysis of nervous system health
Multiplex protein microarray technology ensures highly accurate detection of a wide range of pathogens and immune responses
Comprehensive assessment of both neurological autoimmunity and infectious triggers
Early detection of neurodegenerative processes before clinical symptoms
Useful for complex neurological cases with unclear etiology

mild_demyelination.title: The Mild Demyelination & Cognitive Pattern
mild_demyelination.case_history: Rachel, a 38-year-old nurse, noticed occasional difficulty finding words during patient handoffs and mild fatigue that worsened throughout her shifts. She experienced subtle coordination issues and brief episodes of visual blurring that resolved within minutes.
mild_demyelination.axon_desc: Consistent Elevated Anti-Myelin Basic Protein (MBP) indicates active ongoing demyelination and stress to the oligodendrocytes. This structural degradation is the primary driver behind her slowed signal conduction.
mild_demyelination.synaptic_desc: The presence of Anti-GABA and Anti-NMDA receptor antibodies suggests an imbalance between excitatory and inhibitory signaling, contributing to her 'brain fog' and word-finding difficulties.
mild_demyelination.insight_desc1: This pattern indicates mild autoimmune activity directed toward neuronal structural components rather than synaptic receptors.
mild_demyelination.insight_desc2: The presence of anti-myelin basic protein antibodies reflects early demyelination and oligodendrocyte stress, consistent with intermittent conduction delays and subtle neurological symptoms.
mild_demyelination.insight_desc3: The absence of anti-GABA, anti-AChR, anti-NMDA, and anti-MOG antibodies suggests that receptor-mediated neurotransmission and broader myelin integrity remain largely preserved, pointing to an early stage of autoimmune involvement.
mild_demyelination.conclusion_desc: Early detection in this 'Mild' phase allows for aggressive neuroprotective strategies and identifying the root cause of the immune trigger before progression to significant white matter damage.
